+# Correction Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-92
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-22_16-56-14+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_TASK_2026-05-22_16-45-11+0300.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_EXEC_2026-05-22_16-52-05+0300.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_EXEC_*.md (new correction-round execution report)
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-92/verification.round0.log
+STATUS: CORRECTION_OPEN
+
+## References
+
+- Task Packet: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_TASK_2026-05-22_16-45-11+0300.md`
+- Latest Execution Report: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_EXEC_2026-05-22_16-52-05+0300.md`
+- Latest Review Report: none; orchestrator verification failed before Codex control review.
+- Prior CORRECT packets in this dispatch: none.
+- Verification failure log: `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-92/verification.round0.log`
+
+## Approved Corrections (Planner Sign-Off)
+
+The Executor MUST act on exactly these corrections - nothing more, nothing
+less. There is no approved source-code repair in this correction round.
+
+1. **Route the out-of-scope verify failure to NEEDS_HUMAN** - Post-execution
+   verification gate finding. Required change: write a new correction-round
+   `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet for `ISSUE-92` that reports the correction as
+   blocked by an out-of-scope verification failure. The report MUST use header
+   `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`,
+   `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, and a non-zero `EXIT_CODE`. The executor's
+   final response marker MUST be `EXEC_STATUS: blocked` and `EXEC_PACKET:`
+   pointing at the new report. Acceptance: the new EXEC report states that no
+   source, test, doc, manifest, schema, script, status, or non-EXEC handoff file
+   was edited, and records the exact failed gate and target:
+   `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` failed with exit 101 because
+   `-p rge-gfx --lib` exited abnormally with
+   `0xc0000005, STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION`.
+2. **Preserve the read-only audit scope** - Task halt condition for
+   correction-round verify failures outside the audit scope. Required change:
+   do not investigate or repair `rge-gfx`, do not run broad source-fix work,
+   and do not touch any source file. Acceptance: `git status --short
+   --untracked-files=no` remains clean for tracked files except for the new
+   correction-round EXEC packet and its allowed sidecar.
+
+## Deferred Findings (NOT Approved for This Round)
+
+The following findings are NOT approved for this correction round. They are
+recorded as latent risks for the human/Planner to carry forward.
+
+1. **`rge-gfx --lib` access violation** - Verification log finding at
+   `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-92/verification.round0.log`: the `rge_gfx` unit-test
+   binary ran the `rge-gfx` lib tests and then exited abnormally with
+   `0xc0000005, STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION`; cargo reported
+   `error: test failed, to rerun pass -p rge-gfx --lib`. Deferred because
+   `crates/gfx/**` is outside the read-only audit scope, and the TASK packet
+   explicitly requires a NEEDS_HUMAN/blocking EXEC report rather than an
+   unrelated source fix when canonical verify fails outside the audit surface.
+   Future trigger / dispatch: create or resume a separate infrastructure or
+   `rge-gfx` stabilization dispatch that may edit `crates/gfx/**` and rerun
+   `cargo test -p rge-gfx --lib -- --nocapture`.
+
+## Updated Acceptance Criteria
+
+The original read-only audit acceptance criteria remain unchanged for the
+already-produced audit content. For this correction round, the only acceptable
+outcome is a new EXEC report that blocks with `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN` because the
+canonical verification failure is outside the task scope. This dispatch cannot
+truthfully pass the canonical gate until the unrelated `rge-gfx` access
+violation is fixed by a separate in-scope dispatch or human-authorized change.
+
+## Re-Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run only these correction-round checks:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before writing the new EXEC report,
+  expected clean tracked output.
+- Read `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-92/verification.round0.log` only as needed to quote
+  the exact out-of-scope failure.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` after writing the new EXEC report,
+  expected clean tracked output apart from the allowed new EXEC packet sidecar
+  behavior.
+
+No cargo build, cargo test, cargo fmt, architecture-lint, source generation, or
+docs generation is approved in this correction round. The orchestrator may rerun
+the canonical verification gate after execution; if it still fails on
+`rge-gfx --lib`, the dispatch remains blocked for human arbitration rather than
+being converted into a source-fix dispatch.
+
+## Halt Conditions (Updated if Any)
+
+Unchanged from the Task Packet, with this explicit correction-round emphasis:
+halt immediately if satisfying the verification gate would require editing
+`crates/gfx/**`, `crates/io-gltf/**`, `crates/io-image/**`,
+`crates/asset-store/**`, `kernel/asset-view/**`, any source/test/doc/manifest,
+or any handoff packet other than the new correction-round EXEC report.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The first EXEC report appears to satisfy the requested read-only audit content,
+but the orchestrator's mandatory post-execution verification gate failed before
+Codex control review could run. The failure is not in the scoped audit surface:
+`cargo test --workspace --all-targets` failed because the `rge-gfx` lib test
+binary exited with `STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION`, while the ISSUE-92 TASK explicitly
+limits the work to a read-only audit of `io-gltf`, `io-image`, `asset-store`,
+`kernel/asset-view`, and the dispatch's own EXEC packet.
+
+Do not repair `rge-gfx` here. The approved correction is protocol routing:
+record the out-of-scope gate failure in a new blocked EXEC report and route to
+the human arbiter for a separate source-fix decision.
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-92
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+NEXT_ROLE: EXECUTOR_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,485 @@
+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-92
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-22_16-52-05+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs — audited (read-only)
+- crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs — audited (read-only)
+- crates/asset-store/src/cache.rs — audited (read-only)
+- crates/asset-store/src/local.rs — audited (read-only)
+- crates/asset-store/src/lib.rs — audited (read-only)
+- kernel/asset-view/src/lib.rs — audited (read-only)
+- kernel/asset-view/src/descriptor.rs — audited (read-only)
+- kernel/asset-view/src/id.rs — audited (read-only)
+- kernel/asset-view/src/registry.rs — audited (read-only)
+- kernel/asset-view/Cargo.toml — audited (read-only)
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_TASK_2026-05-22_16-45-11+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- None. This is an audit-only dispatch (TASK `MAY edit: None`).
+
+### Tests
+
+- None.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None outside this EXEC packet.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+No source / test / doc / manifest / schema / lint / script / status edits were
+performed. The only new filesystem artefact is this EXEC packet
+(`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_EXEC_2026-05-22_16-52-05+0300.md`) plus the sidecar
+created on `-Finalize`.
+
+## Audit Evidence Inventory
+
+### io-gltf typed asset-family cache (`crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs`)
+
+- Trait declared `crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs:28` as
+  `pub trait Cache` with **ten** typed methods (lines 30-56): per-family
+  `insert_*` / `get_*` pairs for `MeshAsset` / `MaterialAsset` /
+  `AnimationClip` / `Skeleton` / `ImageAsset`. Handle types are the
+  per-family content-hash newtypes from `crate::handles`.
+- Insert shape: takes the owned asset and returns the family-specific
+  handle, e.g. `fn insert_mesh(&mut self, asset: MeshAsset) -> MeshHandle`
+  (`cache_stub.rs:30`). Implementations call `asset.content_hash()`
+  (`cache_stub.rs:112,121,130,139,148`); BLAKE3 derivation lives on the
+  asset types, not on the cache.
+- Get shape: returns a **borrowed** `Option<&MeshAsset>` /
+  `Option<&MaterialAsset>` / `Option<&AnimationClip>` /
+  `Option<&Skeleton>` / `Option<&ImageAsset>` (`cache_stub.rs:32,37,42,47,56`).
+  No `Result`, no I/O error path.
+- Mutability: insert takes `&mut self`; get takes `&self`. Single-threaded
+  by signature.
+- Reference impl `MemoryCache` (`cache_stub.rs:63`) is a struct of five
+  `std::collections::HashMap<H, A>` fields (`cache_stub.rs:64-69`), in-process
+  only, no persistence, no eviction, no size cap, no LRU.
+- Surface exported as the crate's public `Cache` / `MemoryCache`
+  (`crates/io-gltf/src/lib.rs:96`); used by every integration test (e.g.
+  `tests/cube_round_trip.rs:13`, `tests/pbr_material_test.rs:13`,
+  `tests/animated_character_test.rs:15`, `tests/uv_cube_test.rs:11`,
+  `tests/textured_cube_test.rs:12`, `tests/textured_uv_cube_test.rs:12`,
+  `tests/smooth_normal_sphere_test.rs:19`) and the export module's own
+  unit tests (`crates/io-gltf/src/export.rs:779-796`,
+  `crates/io-gltf/src/import.rs:166-204`). It is the live working cache
+  for io-gltf, not a dead stub.
+- The leading file comment (`cache_stub.rs:2-12`) names W16 as the
+  successor and states "When W16 lands, this file is deleted and
+  `crate::Cache` becomes a re-export of `rge_asset_store::Cache`".
+
+### io-image byte-blob stub (`crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs`)
+
+- The full extent of io-image's cache surface is the single module
+  `pub mod asset_store_stub;` (`crates/io-image/src/lib.rs:62`). There
+  is no other cache-shaped trait in `crates/io-image/src/**`: a search
+  of the crate for `cache|Cache|asset_store` produces hits only inside
+  `asset_store_stub.rs` plus the `pub mod` line in `lib.rs`. **No other
+  io-image cache surface exists.**
+- `pub type AssetId = Vec<u8>;` (`asset_store_stub.rs:20`) — an opaque
+  byte-vec, explicitly *not* content-addressed; the doc-string promises
+  `blake3:<hex>` "in the real crate" but the stub itself never hashes.
+- `pub trait Cache: Send + Sync` (`asset_store_stub.rs:23`) exposes only
+  `put(&self, blob: Vec<u8>) -> AssetId`, `get(&self, id: &AssetId) ->
+  Option<Vec<u8>>`, and `has(&self, id: &AssetId) -> bool`
+  (`asset_store_stub.rs:25-29`). Note: `put` takes `&self` (interior
+  mutability), unlike `asset-store::Cache::put(&mut self, ...)`.
+- Reference impl `MemoryCache` (`asset_store_stub.rs:35`) uses
+  `Arc<Mutex<HashMap<AssetId, Vec<u8>>>>` plus an `Arc<Mutex<u64>>`
+  counter (`asset_store_stub.rs:36-37`). Keys are **synthesised from a
+  counter + blob length** (`asset_store_stub.rs:49-58`) — explicitly not
+  content-addressed, so two identical blobs produce two distinct ids
+  (the opposite of `asset-store::Cache`'s idempotence contract at
+  `crates/asset-store/src/cache.rs:31-34`). No persistence, no
+  eviction, no size cap, no LRU.
+- `pub mod asset_store_stub;` (`crates/io-image/src/lib.rs:62`) is the
+  only mention of the module in the crate. No other file in
+  `crates/io-image/src/**` or `crates/io-image/tests/**` imports
+  `asset_store_stub`. The stub has **zero in-tree consumers** beyond
+  its own unit tests (`asset_store_stub.rs:76-93`). The doc-string
+  (`asset_store_stub.rs:1-11`) explicitly frames it as a placeholder
+  awaiting W16 re-export.
+
+### rge-asset-store byte-blob production cache (`crates/asset-store/src/cache.rs`, `local.rs`, `lib.rs`)
+
+- `Bytes = Vec<u8>` alias (`cache.rs:25`).
+- `pub trait Cache` (`cache.rs:41`) exposes the byte-blob contract:
+  `get(&self, id: &AssetId) -> Result<Option<Bytes>, CacheError>`
+  (`cache.rs:51`), `put(&mut self, bytes: Bytes) -> Result<AssetId,
+  CacheError>` (`cache.rs:61`), `evict_lru(&mut self, max_bytes: u64) ->
+  Result<(), CacheError>` (`cache.rs:71`), plus `total_size`, `len`,
+  `is_empty` (`cache.rs:77-86`). All fallible operations return
+  `CacheError` (`crates/asset-store/src/lib.rs:64-76`) — variants
+  `Io(String)` and `BadAssetId(String)`.
+- Idempotence + content-addressed-id + LRU-cap contract spelled out in
+  the trait doc (`cache.rs:29-40`): `put(b)` twice yields the same id
+  and one logical entry; `get(put(b))` round-trips bytes; id depends
+  only on input bytes via `AssetId::from_bytes` (delegated to
+  `rge-kernel-asset::AssetId`, BLAKE3-keyed —
+  `crates/asset-store/src/asset_id.rs:10`).
+- Test fake `InMemoryCache` (`cache.rs:100`) with `BTreeMap<AssetId,
+  (Bytes, u64)>` plus a monotonic `tick` counter (`cache.rs:103-110`).
+  Hash derivation at insert: `AssetId::from_bytes(&bytes)`
+  (`cache.rs:138`). Deterministic LRU eviction (`cache.rs:152-170`).
+- Production impl `LocalCache` (`crates/asset-store/src/local.rs:57`):
+  filesystem-backed, sharded by 2-char hex prefix
+  (`local.rs:114,196-211`), atomic temp+rename writes
+  (`local.rs:202-217`), plain-ASCII `.index` recency file
+  (`local.rs:53,290-338`), `Drop`-flushed (`local.rs:276-284`),
+  reconciliation pass on `open` (`local.rs:368-420`). Single-threaded
+  by contract; `RefCell` for interior tick / index mutability under
+  `&self` `get` (`local.rs:62-67,138-142`). `Cache::put` is `&mut
+  self` (`local.rs:173`), `Cache::get` is `&self` (`local.rs:146`).
+- Public surface (`crates/asset-store/src/lib.rs:50-53`) re-exports
+  `AssetId`, `Cache`, `Bytes`, `InMemoryCache`, `LocalCache`, plus
+  `DepGraph` for invalidation cascades. **No other workspace crate
+  currently depends on `rge-asset-store`**: `rg -l 'rge-asset-store'`
+  on `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock`/code returns only its own crate,
+  documentation, the architecture-lint config, and the W16 task plan.
+  Neither `crates/io-gltf/Cargo.toml` nor `crates/io-image/Cargo.toml`
+  has a `rge-asset-store` dependency line.
+
+### kernel/asset-view substrate (`kernel/asset-view/src/lib.rs`, `descriptor.rs`, `id.rs`, `registry.rs`)
+
+- `lib.rs:1-49` frames the crate as a **descriptor / id / registry
+  vocabulary substrate** with an explicit NON-GOALS list: no WASM
+  linear-memory mapping, no `unsafe` zero-copy, **no buffer / allocation
+  ownership**, no residency or streaming policy, no GPU upload, no I/O
+  scheduling, no observer pattern, **no `kernel/asset` integration**
+  ("v0 does NOT tie `AssetViewId` to `kernel/asset::AssetId`",
+  `lib.rs:35-36`).
+- `AssetViewId` (`id.rs:18`) is a caller-supplied opaque `[u8; 16]` with
+  no derivation today; future derivation `(asset_id, view_kind,
+  byte_offset, byte_len)` is documented as a future dispatch
+  (`id.rs:11-13`) but explicitly not wired now.
+- `ViewKind` (`descriptor.rs:21`) has the single variant `Placeholder`,
+  marked `#[non_exhaustive]`. `ViewDescriptor` (`descriptor.rs:36`)
+  carries `id + kind + byte_len` only — `byte_len` is "informational in
+  v0" (`descriptor.rs:34,41-43`).
+- `AssetViewRegistry` (`registry.rs:41`) is a passive `BTreeMap<AssetViewId,
+  ViewDescriptor>` with `register` / `unregister` / `lookup` / `contains`
+  / `iter` / `len` / `clear` (`registry.rs:48-106`). It owns no buffers,
+  no GPU state, no IO state (`registry.rs:8-12`).
+- `kernel/asset-view/Cargo.toml:13-16` lists only `serde` (and
+  `serde_json` as a dev-dep). **No `rge-asset-store`, no `rge-io-gltf`,
+  no `rge-io-image`, no `rge-kernel-asset` dependency.**
+- Workspace consumers: `rg -l 'rge-kernel-asset-view|rge_kernel_asset_view|
+  AssetViewRegistry|AssetViewId|ViewDescriptor'` outside `kernel/asset-view`
+  matches **zero real consumers**. The hits in `crates/gfx/**` and
+  `crates/editor-shell/**` are all `wgpu::TextureViewDescriptor` (e.g.
+  `crates/gfx/src/target.rs:68`, `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs:363`),
+  unrelated to the kernel substrate. The single non-wgpu hit in
+  `crates/cad-core/src/topology/face_id/mod.rs:56` is a **doc comment**
+  noting that `FaceId` "Mirrors `kernel/asset-view::AssetViewId`" — it
+  is shape-inspired, not a code dependency. Today `kernel/asset-view`
+  has no callers anywhere in the tree.
+
+## Comparison Table
+
+| Aspect                          | `io-gltf::cache_stub::Cache` / `MemoryCache`                                                            | `io-image::asset_store_stub::Cache` / `MemoryCache`                                  | `asset-store::Cache` / `InMemoryCache` / `LocalCache`                                                                                                            |
+|---------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
+| Key / id type                   | Five distinct typed handles (`MeshHandle`, `MaterialHandle`, `AnimationHandle`, `SkeletonHandle`, `ImageHandle`); each is BLAKE3 of the asset's canonical bytes via `asset.content_hash()` | `Vec<u8>` (opaque blob, **not** content-addressed; counter+length-derived in stub)   | `rge_kernel_asset::AssetId` (BLAKE3 32-byte digest, content-addressed, string-form `blake3:<hex>` parseable)                                                      |
+| Put / insert shape              | Per-family typed: `insert_mesh(MeshAsset) -> MeshHandle`, …, `insert_image(ImageAsset) -> ImageHandle`; takes `&mut self`; infallible; idempotent via `entry().or_insert()` on a content-hash key | `put(&self, Vec<u8>) -> AssetId`; takes `&self` (interior `Mutex`); infallible; **non-idempotent** (counter-derived id changes per call) | `put(&mut self, Bytes) -> Result<AssetId, CacheError>`; takes `&mut self`; fallible (I/O); idempotent (same bytes → same `AssetId`, second call no-op storage-wise) |
+| Get shape                       | Per-family typed: `get_mesh(&MeshHandle) -> Option<&MeshAsset>`, …; **borrowed** view; infallible      | `get(&self, &AssetId) -> Option<Vec<u8>>`; **owned clone** of bytes; infallible      | `get(&self, &AssetId) -> Result<Option<Bytes>, CacheError>`; **owned clone** of bytes; fallible (I/O / read errors distinct from "not present")                  |
+| Error handling                  | None; all ops infallible                                                                               | None; all ops infallible (`Mutex` poisoning is `expect`ed)                          | `CacheError { Io(String), BadAssetId(String) }`; `Result<…, CacheError>` on every fallible op; trait doc distinguishes `Ok(None)` (absent) from `Err` (I/O)       |
+| Mutability / threading          | `&mut self` on insert, `&self` on get; **not** `Send`/`Sync`-bound; single-threaded by signature       | `Send + Sync` required; interior mutability via `Arc<Mutex<…>>`                      | Single-threaded by contract (`cache.rs:73-77` calls out external sync requirement for cross-thread use); `LocalCache` uses `RefCell` internally; intended wrap in `Arc<Mutex<…>>` for sharing |
+| Persistence behavior            | In-process only; lost on drop                                                                          | In-process only; lost on drop                                                        | `InMemoryCache`: in-process. `LocalCache`: filesystem-backed at `<root>/<2-hex>/<full-hash>` with `.index` recency file flushed on `Drop`; reopen-survives        |
+| LRU / eviction behavior         | None; no eviction, no size cap                                                                          | None; no eviction, no size cap                                                       | `evict_lru(&mut self, max_bytes: u64) -> Result<(), CacheError>`; monotonic-tick LRU recency bumped on every `get`/`put`; deterministic eviction order across runs |
+| Typed asset-family handling     | **Yes** — the trait is structurally five-headed; consumers look up a `&MeshAsset`/`&MaterialAsset`/etc. directly without decoding bytes | No — single byte-blob lane; type semantics live entirely in the caller               | No — single byte-blob lane; type semantics live in callers (cooker / importer chooses encoding)                                                                   |
+| Current consumers / tests       | Used by every io-gltf integration test (`tests/cube_round_trip.rs`, `pbr_material_test.rs`, `animated_character_test.rs`, `uv_cube_test.rs`, `textured_cube_test.rs`, `textured_uv_cube_test.rs`, `smooth_normal_sphere_test.rs`) and the importer / exporter's own `#[cfg(test)]` modules. Live working cache. | Zero in-tree consumers outside its own unit tests. Module is `pub` but no other io-image src file or test imports it. | No in-tree consumers of the workspace `rge-asset-store` crate today — its own `tests/dedup_test.rs`, `eviction_test.rs`, `layout_test.rs`, and internal unit tests are the only callers. Neither io-gltf nor io-image yet depends on it.
+
+## Five-Question Preflight Answer Block
+
+1. **What cache surfaces exist today in `io-gltf` and `io-image`, and how do
+   they differ from `rge-asset-store::Cache` plus `LocalCache`?**
+
+   Two locally-owned stubs, structurally **not the same shape** as
+   `rge-asset-store::Cache`:
+
+   - `io-gltf::cache_stub::Cache` (`crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs:28`)
+     is a **typed asset-family cache** with five `insert_*`/`get_*` pairs
+     keyed by per-family content-hash handles (`MeshHandle` / `MaterialHandle`
+     / `AnimationHandle` / `SkeletonHandle` / `ImageHandle`). Get returns a
+     **borrowed** `Option<&T>` of the decoded asset; insert is infallible
+     and `&mut self`. Backed by an in-process `HashMap`-of-typed-assets
+     `MemoryCache` (`cache_stub.rs:63`).
+   - `io-image::asset_store_stub::Cache`
+     (`crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs:23`) is a **byte-blob
+     cache** with `put(&self, Vec<u8>) -> AssetId`, `get(&self, &AssetId)
+     -> Option<Vec<u8>>`, `has(...)`. Keys are an opaque `Vec<u8>`
+     (`asset_store_stub.rs:20`), **not content-addressed** — the stub
+     `MemoryCache` derives ids from a counter + blob length
+     (`asset_store_stub.rs:49-58`), so two identical blobs collide on
+     content but not on id (the opposite of the asset-store contract).
+     The module is `pub mod` but unconsumed in-crate.
+
+   `rge-asset-store::Cache` (`crates/asset-store/src/cache.rs:41`) is a
+   **byte-blob cache** with `Result`-wrapped fallible `get`/`put`,
+   `evict_lru`, `total_size`, `len`, `is_empty`. Keys are
+   `rge_kernel_asset::AssetId` (BLAKE3 32-byte digest, content-addressed,
+   string-form `blake3:<hex>`). `put` is idempotent: same bytes → same
+   id, second call no-op storage-wise. `LocalCache`
+   (`crates/asset-store/src/local.rs:57`) is the production
+   filesystem-backed impl with deterministic monotonic-tick LRU,
+   atomic temp+rename writes, `.index` recency file, reopen-survives
+   reconciliation.
+
+   Net: `io-gltf`'s stub is **typed and borrowing**; `asset-store` is
+   **untyped byte-blob and owning-on-get with `Result`**. The
+   `io-image` stub is closer in shape to `asset-store` (single byte
+   lane) but loses content-addressed determinism and has no consumers
+   forcing the question yet.
+
+2. **Which `io-*` cache surfaces should route through `rge-asset-store`,
+   and which should remain local or test-only?**
+
+   - **`io-image::asset_store_stub`** is the candidate to be deleted in
+     favour of `rge-asset-store::Cache`. It has zero in-tree consumers
+     beyond its own unit tests (`crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs:76-93`),
+     its stub `MemoryCache` is non-content-addressed and explicitly
+     placeholder, and its trait shape (byte-blob `get`/`put`/`has`) is
+     a near-subset of `asset-store::Cache`. The doc-string already
+     names the migration (`asset_store_stub.rs:1-11`). Replacing it
+     does not require touching any io-image codec or test fixture.
+   - **`io-gltf::cache_stub::Cache` / `MemoryCache`** should **not** be
+     swapped wholesale to `asset-store::Cache` directly. The two traits
+     are structurally incompatible: io-gltf's surface is typed
+     per-family with **borrowed-return** get (`Option<&MeshAsset>`,
+     `cache_stub.rs:32`), while asset-store's is byte-blob with
+     **owned-return-and-fallible** get (`Result<Option<Bytes>,
+     CacheError>`, `cache.rs:51`). A real integration would have to
+     either (a) introduce a `TypedCache` adapter that serialises each
+     asset family to canonical bytes for `asset-store::Cache::put` and
+     deserialises plus caches on `get`, or (b) keep `cache_stub` as a
+     thin owning typed-decoded layer above an asset-store byte-blob
+     backing store. Either is a non-trivial design call that belongs
+     to a future dispatch, not the smallest-next-step.
+   - The remainder (per-codec parsing, mip generation, importer scene
+     building) is stateless format adaptation and should stay local —
+     it owns no cache state to route.
+
+3. **Does `kernel/asset-view` become a genuine consumer once `io-gltf`
+   binds to `rge-asset-store`, or does it remain a future descriptor
+   substrate?**
+
+   **It remains a future descriptor substrate.** `kernel/asset-view`
+   today is a passive vocabulary substrate: `AssetViewRegistry` is a
+   `BTreeMap`-backed lookup of `ViewDescriptor { id, kind, byte_len }`
+   (`kernel/asset-view/src/registry.rs:41-46`), `ViewKind` has a single
+   `Placeholder` variant (`descriptor.rs:21-24`), and the crate's own
+   NON-GOALS list explicitly excludes buffer ownership, WASM
+   linear-memory mapping, `kernel/asset` integration, residency, GPU
+   upload, and IO scheduling (`lib.rs:13-39`). It has **zero
+   non-self consumers**: `rg` for `AssetViewRegistry` /
+   `AssetViewId` / `ViewDescriptor` outside the crate finds only
+   `wgpu::TextureViewDescriptor` matches in `crates/gfx/**` and
+   `crates/editor-shell/**` plus one documentation cross-reference in
+   `crates/cad-core/src/topology/face_id/mod.rs:56`. Its `Cargo.toml`
+   lists only `serde`/`serde_json` — no `rge-asset-store`, no
+   `rge-io-gltf`, no `rge-kernel-asset`.
+
+   Binding `io-image` (or even `io-gltf`) to `rge-asset-store::Cache`
+   does **not** add pressure on `asset-view`. The cache trait yields
+   owned `Bytes` (`crates/asset-store/src/cache.rs:51`), it does not
+   produce read-only borrowed slices, and the importer / exporter
+   currently has no descriptor-vocabulary consumer waiting for it.
+   Genuine consumer pressure would arrive when (a) a real `ViewKind`
+   variant lands (e.g. `MeshVertices` / `Texture2D`) backed by a
+   `kernel/asset-streaming` residency owner that produces the read-only
+   slice, or (b) a WASM-bridge call site needs to surface a registered
+   descriptor to a script. Neither exists today.
+
+4. **What is the smallest next dispatch that swaps one `io-*` cache to
+   the asset-store `Cache` trait without churning the kernel cavity?**
+
+   Delete `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs` and the
+   `pub mod asset_store_stub;` re-export at
+   `crates/io-image/src/lib.rs:62`, and add an `rge-asset-store =
+   { workspace = true }` dependency line to `crates/io-image/Cargo.toml`
+   so io-image callers that *would* have reached for the stub get
+   `rge_asset_store::Cache` / `LocalCache` instead. Because the stub
+   has zero in-tree consumers today (`rg -n "asset_store_stub"
+   crates/io-image` finds only the stub itself and the `pub mod` line),
+   the swap is mechanical and surface-preserving from io-image's
+   perspective: nothing in io-image's tests, codecs, or `Image`
+   pipeline ever decodes through the stub. The dispatch is the
+   smallest possible because it is one module removal plus one
+   manifest line, with no adapter code, no asset-family typing
+   decision, no kernel substrate edits, and no test churn beyond
+   removing the stub's own unit tests in `asset_store_stub.rs:76-93`.
+
+   The `io-gltf` integration is **not** the smallest next step. Its
+   typed five-family borrowed-return trait shape is structurally
+   incompatible with `asset-store::Cache`'s byte-blob owning-return
+   shape; a real swap requires either a typed adapter or a
+   serialisation contract per asset family. That is a design dispatch,
+   not a wiring dispatch, and the audit found no concrete consumer
+   pressure that forces it ahead of the io-image step.
+
+5. **What must that next dispatch explicitly not touch, and what gates
+   or halt conditions should it carry?**
+
+   **Must not touch:**
+   - `crates/io-gltf/**` (out of scope for the smallest io-image swap).
+   - `crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs` and the io-gltf typed `Cache`
+     trait — no replacement, no adapter, no re-export change.
+   - `kernel/asset-view/**` — no descriptor / id / registry edits, no
+     new `ViewKind` variants, no `rge-asset-store` dep in
+     `kernel/asset-view/Cargo.toml`.
+   - `kernel/asset/**` and `rge-kernel-asset` re-export wiring beyond
+     what `rge-asset-store` already pulls.
+   - Any io-image codec source (`png.rs`, `jpeg.rs`, `exr.rs`,
+     `hdr.rs`, `mip_chain.rs`, `image_data.rs`, `format_detect.rs`,
+     `error.rs`) — the swap is a stub-removal + manifest edit only.
+   - Workspace lint configuration, CI workflows, and architecture
+     lints beyond a possible single-line `forbidden_dep` update if the
+     existing rule requires it (and even that should be its own minor
+     dispatch if it surfaces).
+   - Any handoff packets other than the dispatch's own
+     `TASK` / `EXEC` (orchestrator-mode) chain.
+
+   **Gates / halt conditions for that dispatch:**
+   - `git status --short --untracked-files=no` clean before and after.
+   - `cargo build -p rge-io-image` green, `cargo test -p rge-io-image
+     --lib --no-fail-fast` green (no test regressions from removing
+     the stub's own unit tests is acceptable, but no other test may
+     start failing).
+   - `cargo build -p rge-asset-store` and `cargo test -p
+     rge-asset-store --lib --no-fail-fast` remain green.
+   - Architecture lint pass (`cargo run -q -p
+     rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0). If the
+     `forbidden_dep` lint at `tools/architecture-lints/src/forbidden_dep.rs`
+     currently lists `rge-asset-store` as off-limits for io-image
+     (the search showed it referenced there), the dispatch must halt
+     and route a Planner decision before changing the lint — that
+     decision is **not** the Executor's call.
+   - Halt if any consumer of `io_image::asset_store_stub` is
+     discovered outside io-image (a re-`rg` at dispatch start). The
+     current audit found none, but the halt makes the dispatch safe
+     against drift between this packet and execution time.
+   - Halt if the swap requires touching `io-gltf`, `kernel/asset-view`,
+     or any cooker / streaming caller. None should — the entire stub
+     is self-contained.
+
+## Recommended Next-Dispatch Shape (prose only — do not create the TASK)
+
+A small W16-adjacent wiring dispatch limited to `crates/io-image/Cargo.toml`,
+`crates/io-image/src/lib.rs`, and `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs`,
+removing the stub and replacing its public surface with a thin
+`pub use rge_asset_store::{Cache, AssetId, LocalCache};` re-export (or
+simply deleting the surface entirely if the audit confirms zero downstream
+imports — current evidence supports outright deletion). Scope is one
+crate; no source-file edits outside the three files above; no test
+fixtures; no kernel cavity touch; no `io-gltf` touch; no
+`kernel/asset-view` touch. The dispatch is the genuinely smallest
+W16-binding step the workspace currently supports because every other
+candidate (typed io-gltf adapter; asset-view consumer wiring; cooker
+plumbing) requires a design decision that belongs to a Planner round,
+not an Executor wiring round.
+
+`kernel/asset-view` is **out of scope** for this follow-on. It would
+become a genuine consumer only when a non-`Placeholder` `ViewKind`
+variant arrives backed by a real residency / streaming owner that
+yields a borrowed slice — neither exists in the tree today, and
+inventing either purely to give `asset-view` something to do would be
+the design-by-policy failure the TASK packet explicitly warns
+against (Constraints §"Do not try to make `asset-view` a consumer by
+policy argument alone").
+
+**Final recommendation: PROCEED** — the io-image stub removal is a
+mechanically-bounded, halt-conditioned, single-crate wiring step with
+no in-tree consumers blocking it. The caller may launch a focused
+follow-on TASK packet at that scope; the `io-gltf` typed-cache
+question and any `kernel/asset-view` consumer-binding question should
+be deferred to separate, design-led dispatches.
+

+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-92
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-22_16-45-11+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/io-gltf/**
+- crates/io-image/**
+- crates/asset-store/**
+- kernel/asset-view/**
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_EXEC_*.md
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Run the GitHub issue #92 read-only preflight for the W16 `rge-asset-store` integration shape. Audit the current cache seams in `crates/io-gltf/` and `crates/io-image/`, compare them against `rge-asset-store`'s `Cache` trait and `LocalCache`, and determine whether `kernel/asset-view` becomes a real consumer or remains a future descriptor substrate. This dispatch produces only an evidence-backed recommendation for the next smallest implementation dispatch; it does not implement the integration.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- None.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `crates/io-gltf/**`
+- `crates/io-image/**`
+- `crates/asset-store/**`
+- `kernel/asset-view/**`
+- Any source file.
+- Any test file.
+- Any documentation file.
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- Workspace dependency declarations, feature flags, lint configuration, CI/workflow files, scripts, schemas, `.gitignore`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, or `change.md`.
+- Any handoff packet other than the single required `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_EXEC_*.md` execution report.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_EXEC_*.md`.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files.
+- New test files.
+- New docs.
+- New fixtures, generated artifacts, screenshots, logs intended for tracking, Cargo entries, new crates, architecture lints, doctrine docs, ADRs, schemas, scripts, or CI/config files.
+- New TASK, REVIEW, CORRECT, or CLOSEOUT packets.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- One `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet under `ai_handoffs/` and no other filesystem changes.
+- A read-only evidence inventory of the cache-related surfaces in:
+  - `crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs`
+  - any `crates/io-image/` cache or asset-store stub surface, including explicit evidence if no broader cache surface exists
+  - `crates/asset-store/src/cache.rs`
+  - `crates/asset-store/src/local.rs`
+  - `crates/asset-store/src/lib.rs`
+  - `kernel/asset-view/src/lib.rs` and any descriptor/id/registry files needed to classify consumer readiness
+- A compact comparison table covering at least: key/id type, put/insert shape, get shape, error handling, mutability/threading expectation, persistence behavior, LRU/eviction behavior, typed asset-family handling, and current consumers/tests.
+- The existing preflight-format 5-question answer block, with exactly these five numbered questions and concise answers:
+  1. What cache surfaces exist today in `io-gltf` and `io-image`, and how do they differ from `rge-asset-store::Cache` plus `LocalCache`?
+  2. Which `io-*` cache surfaces should route through `rge-asset-store`, and which should remain local or test-only?
+  3. Does `kernel/asset-view` become a genuine consumer once `io-gltf` binds to `rge-asset-store`, or does it remain a future descriptor substrate?
+  4. What is the smallest next dispatch that swaps one `io-*` cache to the asset-store `Cache` trait without churning the kernel cavity?
+  5. What must that next dispatch explicitly not touch, and what gates or halt conditions should it carry?
+- A recommended next dispatch shape only as prose inside the EXEC packet. Do not create the next TASK packet.
+- A final recommendation of `PROCEED`, `DEFER`, or `NEEDS_HUMAN` for follow-on implementation, with the caller left to decide whether to launch it.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The dispatch is audit-only. No source, tests, docs, manifests, scripts, schemas, status files, or non-EXEC handoff packets are edited or added.
+- Exactly one new EXEC packet is produced for this dispatch.
+- The EXEC packet footer contains exactly one line-anchored `HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE` marker and routes `NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI`, unless a halt condition requires `HANDOFF_STATUS: BLOCKED` or `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- The EXEC packet includes the required 5-question answer block with all five questions present and answered.
+- The EXEC packet cites concrete file/line evidence for the `io-gltf::cache_stub::MemoryCache` surface and its `Cache` trait, any `io-image` cache surface found, `asset-store::Cache`, `LocalCache`, and `kernel/asset-view`'s current substrate role.
+- The comparison distinguishes the typed `io-gltf` asset-family cache shape from the byte-blob `asset-store::Cache` shape rather than treating them as identical by name.
+- The recommendation states which `io-*` crate should be the first integration target, why that target is smallest, and which crates must be excluded from the first follow-on dispatch.
+- The recommendation explicitly states whether `kernel/asset-view` is in scope for the follow-on implementation. If the answer is no, explain what concrete consumer pressure would make it genuine later.
+- No cargo build/test/fmt gate is required or run as a success condition for this read-only preflight. If the Executor runs any cargo command voluntarily, document it without using it to expand scope.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` remains clean before and after the audit.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not implement W16 asset-store integration.
+- Do not replace `io-gltf::cache_stub::MemoryCache`.
+- Do not replace any `io-image` stub or cache surface.
+- Do not add `rge-asset-store` dependencies to `rge-io-gltf` or `rge-io-image`.
+- Do not edit or expand `kernel/asset-view`.
+- Do not create adapters, traits, wrappers, tests, fixtures, docs, lints, ADRs, or Cargo changes.
+- Do not try to make `asset-view` a consumer by policy argument alone; classify it from present code and concrete dataflow pressure.
+- Do not commit or push.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document each applicable gate in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before audit work, expected empty output.
+- Inventory the four scoped directories. Use `rg --files crates/io-gltf crates/io-image crates/asset-store kernel/asset-view` if `rg` is available; otherwise use the PowerShell equivalent `Get-ChildItem -Recurse -File -LiteralPath crates/io-gltf,crates/io-image,crates/asset-store,kernel/asset-view`.
+- Search the scoped directories for cache/id surfaces. Use `rg -n "MemoryCache|asset_store_stub|trait Cache|struct LocalCache|impl Cache|AssetId|AssetView" crates/io-gltf crates/io-image crates/asset-store kernel/asset-view` if `rg` is available; otherwise use an equivalent `Select-String` command and record that fallback.
+- Read the specific cache and asset-view files needed for evidence; cite file/line references in the EXEC.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` after writing the EXEC packet, expected empty output for tracked files.
+
+No cargo build, cargo test, cargo fmt, architecture-lint run, source generation, or docs generation is required for this audit-only dispatch.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt and write an EXEC packet instead of expanding scope if any of the following occur:
+
+- Any source, test, doc, manifest, script, schema, status, or non-EXEC handoff file would need to be edited to answer the audit.
+- The cache relationship cannot be classified from the scoped code and manifests.
+- The smallest next dispatch cannot be described without first making a design decision that belongs to the human or Planner.
+- The audit reveals that `kernel/asset-view` would need source changes before a truthful consumer classification can be made.
+- Any tracked file is modified before or during the dispatch. Do not repair it; report the state and halt.
+- A correction round is requested for a verify failure outside this audit scope. In that case the Executor MUST NOT fix the failure, MUST NOT edit unrelated files, and MUST write an EXEC report with `EXEC_STATUS: blocked`, header `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, and the exact out-of-scope failure. This is mandatory even if the failure appears easy to fix.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+This task is intentionally narrower than an implementation dispatch. Issue #92 asks for the decision record that should precede W16 integration: whether the typed `io-gltf` cache stub should become an adapter over the byte-oriented `rge-asset-store::Cache`, whether `io-image` has an independently meaningful cache seam, and whether `kernel/asset-view` is a real immediate consumer or remains a future descriptor vocabulary. The caller will decide the follow-on dispatch from the audit; the Executor should not create that dispatch.
+
+Under the orchestrator protocol, no `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packet is expected from this TASK. The only new packet produced by execution should be the `ISSUE-92_EXEC_*.md` report.
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-92
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+NEXT_ROLE: EXECUTOR_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---
